rationale: The insertion index for `k` into a strictly-ordered, k-free vector is unique. I prove `r1 == r2` by case-splitting on `r1_ vs r2_`: in `r1_ < r2_`, instantiating sortedness of `post1_self_@` at `(r1_, r1_+1)` gives `k < s[r1_]`, while instantiating sortedness of `post2_self_@` at `(r1_, r2_)` gives `s[r1_] < k`; `K::cmp_properties()` asymmetry contradicts these. Symmetric for `r2_ < r1_`. With `r1 == r2`, Seq extensionality (`=~=`) closes the views; every step is an `assert` Verus must verify.
